meaning: adj.无实质的,不坚固的,薄弱的
examples: His arms enclosed only on unsubstantial image.
Post-marketing drug assessment is now onset in our country, and the research is unsubstantial.
Reasonableness, on the contrary, just consists in embracing within itself these opposites as unsubstantial elements.
The seven judges agreed that the 15-year threshold was "not an unsubstantial period of time" and that the UK Parliament had considered the matter in some detail.
